title: Conventional Commits and GitHub Actions Workflow
description: Write a proper conventional commit message with a breaking change, and create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs tests on push.
starterCode:
# Task 1: Write a GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)
# Requirements:
#   - Trigger on push to main AND on pull_request targeting main
#   - Check out the repository code
#   - Set up Node.js version 20 with npm cache
#   - Install dependencies with npm ci
#   - Run the test suite with npm test
#
# Task 2 (as a YAML comment below the workflow):
#   Show an example conventional commit message that includes:
#   - A breaking change using the ! suffix on the type
#   - A BREAKING CHANGE footer explaining what changed
#   - A closes/issue reference

# Write your workflow YAML below:
solutionCode:
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

# Example conventional commit with breaking change:
# feat(api)!: remove deprecated /v1/users endpoint
#
# BREAKING CHANGE: The /v1/users endpoint has been removed.
# Migrate to /v2/users which supports pagination and filtering.
#
# Closes #142
hints: The 'on:' key defines triggers — use 'push:' and 'pull_request:' as sub-keys, each with 'branches: [main]'
Use actions/checkout@v4 (not v3) — always pin to the latest major version
npm ci is preferred over npm install in CI — it's faster and uses package-lock.json exactly
For breaking changes use the ! suffix: feat(scope)!: description, AND add a BREAKING CHANGE: footer

validationRules.errorMessage: The YAML must be valid syntax — check indentation, colons, and list formatting
Use actions/checkout@v4 to check out the repository code
Trigger the workflow on push events using the 'on: push:' syntax
Include an example conventional commit comment with a breaking change — use feat(scope)!: or a BREAKING CHANGE: footer

simulatedOutput.success:
YAML syntax valid ✓

Workflow analysis:
  Trigger: push to main, pull_request to main ✓
  Jobs: test (ubuntu-latest) ✓
  Steps:
    ✓ actions/checkout@v4
    ✓ actions/setup-node@v4 (Node 20, npm cache)
    ✓ npm ci
    ✓ npm test

Conventional commit example validated:
  Type: feat  Scope: api
  Breaking change: ✓ (! suffix + BREAKING CHANGE footer)
  Semantic version bump: MAJOR (1.x.x → 2.0.0)

This workflow will appear under GitHub Actions → CI tab.
simulatedOutput.failure: ERROR: Check that your YAML is valid, includes actions/checkout@v4, actions/setup-node, a push trigger, and a conventional commit example with a breaking change notation.
